get.description: Get the current DNS record usage and quota for an account. May include internal DNS usage and quota.

4XX.description: Get DNS Record Usage response failure

get.summary: Get DNS Record Usage for Account

get.description:
Lists, searches, sorts, and filters your zones. Listing zones across more than 500 accounts
is currently not allowed.

schema.description:
A domain name. Optional filter operators can be provided to extend refine the search:
  * `equal` (default)
  * `not_equal`
  * `starts_with`
  * `ends_with`
  * `contains`
  * `starts_with_case_sensitive`
  * `ends_with_case_sensitive`
  * `contains_case_sensitive`
Specify a zone status to filter by.
Filter by an account ID.

An account Name. Optional filter operators can be provided to extend refine the search:
  * `equal` (default)
  * `not_equal`
  * `starts_with`
  * `ends_with`
  * `contains`
  * `starts_with_case_sensitive`
  * `ends_with_case_sensitive`
  * `contains_case_sensitive`
Page number of paginated results.
Number of zones per page.
Field to order zones by.
Direction to order zones.
Whether to match all search requirements or at least one (any).

x-api-token-group: Access: Organizations, Identity Providers, and Groups Write

patch.description: Edits a zone. Only one zone property can be changed at a time.

plan.description:
(Deprecated) Please use the `/zones/{zone_id}/subscription` API
to update a zone's plan. Changing this value will create/cancel
associated subscriptions. To view available plans for this zone,
see Zone Plans.

type.description:
A full zone implies that DNS is hosted with Cloudflare. A partial
zone is typically a partner-hosted zone or a CNAME setup. This
parameter is only available to Enterprise customers or if it has
been explicitly enabled on a zone.

get.description: List, search, sort, and filter a zones' DNS records.

schema.description: Exact value of the DNS record name. This is a convenience alias for `name.exact`.
Exact value of the DNS record name. Name filters are case-insensitive.
Substring of the DNS record name. Name filters are case-insensitive.
Prefix of the DNS record name. Name filters are case-insensitive.
Suffix of the DNS record name. Name filters are case-insensitive.
Exact value of the DNS record content. This is a convenience alias for `content.exact`.
Exact value of the DNS record content. Content filters are case-insensitive.
Substring of the DNS record content. Content filters are case-insensitive.
Prefix of the DNS record content. Content filters are case-insensitive.
Suffix of the DNS record content. Content filters are case-insensitive.
Exact value of the DNS record comment. This is a convenience alias for `comment.exact`.
If this parameter is present, only records *with* a comment are returned.
If this parameter is present, only records *without* a comment are returned.
Exact value of the DNS record comment. Comment filters are case-insensitive.
Substring of the DNS record comment. Comment filters are case-insensitive.
Prefix of the DNS record comment. Comment filters are case-insensitive.
Suffix of the DNS record comment. Comment filters are case-insensitive.

Condition on the DNS record tag.

Parameter values can be of the form `<tag-name>:<tag-value>` to search for an exact `name:value` pair, or just `<tag-name>` to search for records with a specific tag name regardless of its value.

This is a convenience shorthand for the more powerful `tag.<predicate>` parameters.
Examples:
- `tag=important` is equivalent to `tag.present=important`
- `tag=team:DNS` is equivalent to `tag.exact=team:DNS`
Name of a tag which must be present on the DNS record. Tag filters are case-insensitive.
Name of a tag which must *not* be present on the DNS record. Tag filters are case-insensitive.
A tag and value, of the form `<tag-name>:<tag-value>`. The API will only return DNS records that have a tag named `<tag-name>` whose value is `<tag-value>`. Tag filters are case-insensitive.
A tag and value, of the form `<tag-name>:<tag-value>`. The API will only return DNS records that have a tag named `<tag-name>` whose value contains `<tag-value>`. Tag filters are case-insensitive.
A tag and value, of the form `<tag-name>:<tag-value>`. The API will only return DNS records that have a tag named `<tag-name>` whose value starts with `<tag-value>`. Tag filters are case-insensitive.
A tag and value, of the form `<tag-name>:<tag-value>`. The API will only return DNS records that have a tag named `<tag-name>` whose value ends with `<tag-value>`. Tag filters are case-insensitive.

post.description:
Create a new DNS record for a zone.

Notes:
- A/AAAA records cannot exist on the same name as CNAME records.
- NS records cannot exist on the same name as any other record type.
- Domain names are always represented in Punycode, even if Unicode
  characters were used when creating the record.

Send a Batch of DNS Record API calls to be executed together.

Notes:
- Although Cloudflare will execute the batched operations in a single database transaction, Cloudflare's distributed KV store must treat each record change as a single key-value pair. This means that the propagation of changes is not atomic. See [the documentation](https://developers.cloudflare.com/dns/manage-dns-records/how-to/batch-record-changes/ "Batch DNS records") for more information.
- The operations you specify within the /batch request body are always executed in the following order:

    - Deletes
    - Patches
    - Puts
    - Posts

get.description:
You can export your [BIND config](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zone_file "Zone file") through this endpoint.

See [the documentation](https://developers.cloudflare.com/dns/manage-dns-records/how-to/import-and-export/ "Import and export records") for more information.

schema.description: Exported BIND zone file.

post.description:
You can upload your [BIND config](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zone_file "Zone file") through this endpoint. It assumes that cURL is called from a location with bind_config.txt (valid BIND config) present.

See [the documentation](https://developers.cloudflare.com/dns/manage-dns-records/how-to/import-and-export/ "Import and export records") for more information.

file.description:
BIND config to import.

**Tip:** When using cURL, a file can be uploaded using `--form 'file=@bind_config.txt'`.

proxied.description:
Whether or not proxiable records should receive the performance and security benefits of Cloudflare.

The value should be either `true` or `false`.

post.description: Scan for common DNS records on your domain and automatically add them to your zone. Useful if you haven't updated your nameservers yet.
post.x-stainless-deprecation-message: This endpoint is deprecated in favor of a new asynchronous version. Please use the [/scan/trigger](https://developers.cloudflare.com/api/resources/dns/subresources/records/methods/scan/trigger) and [/scan/review](https://developers.cloudflare.com/api/resources/dns/subresources/records/methods/scan/review) endpoints instead.

get.description: Retrieves the list of DNS records discovered up to this point by the asynchronous scan. These records are temporary until explicitly accepted or rejected via `POST /scan/review`. Additional records may be discovered by the scan later.

post.description: Accept or reject DNS records found by the DNS records scan. Accepted records will be permanently added to the zone, while rejected records will be permanently deleted.

Initiates an asynchronous scan for common DNS records on your domain. Note that this **does not** automatically add records to your zone. The scan runs in the background, and results can be reviewed later using the `/scan/review` endpoints. Useful if you haven't updated your nameservers yet.

4XX.description: Trigger DNS Records Scan response failure

get.description: Get the current DNS record usage for a zone, including the number of records and the quota limit.

4XX.description: Get DNS Record Usage response failure

patch.description:
Update an existing DNS record.

Notes:
- A/AAAA records cannot exist on the same name as CNAME records.
- NS records cannot exist on the same name as any other record type.
- Domain names are always represented in Punycode, even if Unicode
  characters were used when creating the record.

put.description:
Overwrite an existing DNS record.

Notes:
- A/AAAA records cannot exist on the same name as CNAME records.
- NS records cannot exist on the same name as any other record type.
- Domain names are always represented in Punycode, even if Unicode
  characters were used when creating the record.

content.description: A valid IPv6 address.

private_routing.description: Enables private network routing to the origin.

content.description: A valid IPv4 address.

private_routing.description: Enables private network routing to the origin.

content.description: Formatted CAA content. See 'data' to set CAA properties.

tag.description: Name of the property controlled by this record (e.g.: issue, issuewild, iodef).

value.description: Value of the record. This field's semantics depend on the chosen tag.

content.description: Formatted CERT content. See 'data' to set CERT properties.
A valid hostname. Must not match the record's name.

flatten_cname.description: If enabled, causes the CNAME record to be resolved externally and the resulting address records (e.g., A and AAAA) to be returned instead of the CNAME record itself. This setting is unavailable for proxied records, since they are always flattened.

content.description: Formatted DNSKEY content. See 'data' to set DNSKEY properties.
Formatted DS content. See 'data' to set DS properties.
Formatted HTTPS content. See 'data' to set HTTPS properties.
Formatted LOC content. See 'data' to set LOC properties.
A valid mail server hostname.
Formatted NAPTR content. See 'data' to set NAPTR properties.
A valid name server host name.
A single Base64-encoded OpenPGP Transferable Public Key (RFC 4880 Section 11.1)
Domain name pointing to the address.
Formatted SMIMEA content. See 'data' to set SMIMEA properties.
Priority, weight, port, and SRV target. See 'data' for setting the individual component values.
Formatted SSHFP content. See 'data' to set SSHFP properties.
Formatted SVCB content. See 'data' to set SVCB properties.
Formatted TLSA content. See 'data' to set TLSA properties.

Text content for the record. The content must consist of quoted "character strings" (RFC 1035), each with a length of up to 255 bytes. Strings exceeding this allowed maximum length are automatically split.

Learn more at <https://www.cloudflare.com/learning/dns/dns-records/dns-txt-record/>.
Formatted URI content. See 'data' to set URI properties.

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service.

page.description: Current page within paginated list of results.

per_page.description: Number of results per page of results.

total_count.description: Total results available without any search parameters.

total_pages.description: The number of total pages in the entire result set.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.
Whether the API call was successful.

dns-records_comment.description: Comments or notes about the DNS record. This field has no effect on DNS responses.

dns-records_direction.description: Direction to order DNS records in.

comment_modified_on.description: When the record comment was last modified. Omitted if there is no comment.

meta.description: Extra Cloudflare-specific information about the record.

modified_on.description: When the record was last modified.

proxiable.description: Whether the record can be proxied by Cloudflare or not.

tags_modified_on.description: When the record tags were last modified. Omitted if there are no tags.

internal_record_quota.description: Maximum number of DNS records allowed across all internal zones in the account. Only present if internal DNS is enabled.

internal_record_usage.description: Current number of DNS records across all internal zones in the account. Only present if internal DNS is enabled.

record_quota.description: Maximum number of DNS records allowed across all public zones in the account. Null if using zone-level quota.

record_usage.description: Current number of DNS records across all public zones in the account.

total_records_parsed.description: Total number of DNS records parsed.

record_quota.description: Maximum number of DNS records allowed for the zone. Null if using account-level quota.

record_usage.description: Current number of DNS records in the zone.

dns-records_match.description: Whether to match all search requirements or at least one (any). If set to `all`, acts like a logical AND between filters. If set to `any`, acts like a logical OR instead. Note that the interaction between tag filters is controlled by the `tag-match` parameter instead.

dns-records_name.description: Complete DNS record name, including the zone name, in Punycode.

dns-records_order.description: Field to order DNS records by.

dns-records_per_page.description: Number of DNS records per page.

dns-records_priority.description: Required for MX and URI records; ignored for other record types (but may still be returned by the API). Records with lower priorities are preferred. This field is to be deprecated in favor of the priority field within the data map.

dns-records_proxied.description: Whether the record is receiving the performance and security benefits of Cloudflare.

dns-records_search.description: Allows searching in multiple properties of a DNS record simultaneously. This parameter is intended for human users, not automation. Its exact behavior is intentionally left unspecified and is subject to change in the future. This parameter works independently of the `match` setting. For automated searches, please use the other available parameters.

ipv4_only.description: When enabled, only A records will be generated, and AAAA records will not be created. This setting is intended for exceptional cases. Note that this option only applies to proxied records and it has no effect on whether Cloudflare communicates with the origin using IPv4 or IPv6.

ipv6_only.description: When enabled, only AAAA records will be generated, and A records will not be created. This setting is intended for exceptional cases. Note that this option only applies to proxied records and it has no effect on whether Cloudflare communicates with the origin using IPv4 or IPv6.

dns-records_tag_match.description: Whether to match all tag search requirements or at least one (any). If set to `all`, acts like a logical AND between tag filters. If set to `any`, acts like a logical OR instead. Note that the regular `match` parameter is still used to combine the resulting condition with other filters that aren't related to tags.

dns-records_tags.description: Custom tags for the DNS record. This field has no effect on DNS responses.

items.description: Individual tag of the form name:value (the name must consist of only letters, numbers, underscores and hyphens)

dns-records_ttl.description: Time To Live (TTL) of the DNS record in seconds. Setting to 1 means 'automatic'. Value must be between 60 and 86400, with the minimum reduced to 30 for Enterprise zones.

success.description: Specifies whether the API call was successful.
Specifies whether the API call was successful.

web3_content_list_entry.description: Specify a content list entry to block.

web3_content_list_entry_content.description: Specify the CID or content path of content to block.

web3_content_list_entry_description.description: Specify an optional description of the content list entry.
web3_content_list_entry_description.example: this is my content list entry

web3_content_list_entry_type.description: Specify the type of content list entry to block.

web3_description.description: Specify an optional description of the hostname.

web3_dnslink.description: Specify the DNSLink value used if the target is ipfs.

web3_identifier.description: Specify the identifier of the hostname.

web3_name.description: Specify the hostname that points to the target gateway via CNAME.

count.description: Specifies the total number of results for the requested service.

page.description: Specifies the current page within paginated list of results.

per_page.description: Specifies the number of results per page of results.

total_count.description: Specifies the total results available without any search parameters.

web3_status.description: Specifies the status of the hostname's activation.

web3_target.description: Specify the target gateway of the hostname.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.
Whether the API call was successful.

zones_name.description: The domain name. Per [RFC 1035](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc1035#section-2.3.4) the overall zone name can be up to 253 characters, with each segment ("label") not exceeding 63 characters.

zones_paused.description:
Indicates whether the zone is only using Cloudflare DNS services. A
true value means the zone will not receive security or performance
benefits.

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service.

page.description: Current page within paginated list of results.

per_page.description: Number of results per page of results.

total_count.description: Total results available without any search parameters.

zones_type.description:
A full zone implies that DNS is hosted with Cloudflare. A partial zone is
typically a partner-hosted zone or a CNAME setup.

zones_vanity_name_servers.description:
An array of domains used for custom name servers. This is only
available for Business and Enterprise plans.

account.description: The account the zone belongs to.

activated_on.description:
The last time proof of ownership was detected and the zone was made
active.

cname_suffix.description:
Allows the customer to use a custom apex.
*Tenants Only Configuration*.

development_mode.description:
The interval (in seconds) from when development mode expires
(positive integer) or last expired (negative integer) for the
domain. If development mode has never been enabled, this value is 0.

cdn_only.description: The zone is only configured for CDN.

custom_certificate_quota.description: Number of Custom Certificates the zone can have.

dns_only.description: The zone is only configured for DNS.

foundation_dns.description: The zone is setup with Foundation DNS.

page_rule_quota.description: Number of Page Rules a zone can have.

phishing_detected.description: The zone has been flagged for phishing.

modified_on.description: When the zone was last modified.

name.description: The domain name. Per [RFC 1035](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc1035#section-2.3.4) the overall zone name can be up to 253 characters, with each segment ("label") not exceeding 63 characters.

name_servers.description: The name servers Cloudflare assigns to a zone.

original_dnshost.description: DNS host at the time of switching to Cloudflare.

original_name_servers.description: Original name servers before moving to Cloudflare.

original_registrar.description: Registrar for the domain at the time of switching to Cloudflare.

permissions.description: Legacy permissions based on legacy user membership information.
permissions.x-stainless-deprecation-message: This has been replaced by Account memberships.

can_subscribe.description: States if the subscription can be activated.

externally_managed.description: If this Zone is managed by another company.

frequency.description: How often the customer is billed.

legacy_discount.description: If the legacy discount applies to this Zone.

legacy_id.description: The legacy name of the plan.

price.description: How much the customer is paying.

plan.x-stainless-deprecation-message:
Please use the `/zones/{zone_id}/subscription` API
to update a zone's plan. Changing this value will create/cancel
associated subscriptions. To view available plans for this zone,
see [Zone Plans](https://developers.cloudflare.com/api/resources/zones/subresources/plans/).

tenant.description: The root organizational unit that this zone belongs to (such as a tenant or organization).

name.description: The name of the Tenant account.

tenant_unit.description: The immediate parent organizational unit that this zone belongs to (such as under a tenant or sub-organization).

vanity_name_servers.description: An array of domains used for custom name servers. This is only available for Business and Enterprise plans.

verification_key.description: Verification key for partial zone setup.
